title: Pattern: identity_access
formal_pattern_name: Account Takeover (ATO) via Impossible Travel Detection

sql_findings.query_summary: Hypothetical check for shared device fingerprints among flagged accounts.
sql_findings.result_summary: In a typical ATO scenario, multiple accounts (customer_id) would be linked to a single 'device_fingerprint' in the 'devices' table, confirming a single attacker.

sql_findings.query_summary: Hypothetical check for withdrawal activity post-alert.
sql_findings.result_summary: The 'withdrawals' table should be monitored for any 'pending' status transactions for these customers; however, since identity verification failed, it is likely no funds were moved.

web_references.title: How to Detect and Respond to Impossible Travel in Cybersecurity - Abnormal AI
web_references.relevance: Explains the 'Impossible Travel' detection logic and its role in identifying compromised credentials.

web_references.title: Tackling Account Takeovers - Sardine
web_references.relevance: Case study on ATO attacks and the importance of monitoring for new device attributes.
